title: Support url without --file parameter for bud
body:
**Description**

As noted by @edsantiago in https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/issues/682, the 'buildah bud {url}' does not work unless '-f' and '.' is used ala 'buildah bud -f {url} .'   Before implementing, verify that the lack of -f and . is supported by Docker.

**Output of `buildah version`:**

Buildah v0.16

comments.body:
Comparison of behaviour on latest commit (2ea3e11):

Docker (works without -f)
```
# docker build https://raw.githubusercontent.com/projectatomic/buildah/master/tests/bud/run-scenarios/Dockerfile.entrypoint-run
Downloading build context from remote url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/projectatomic/buildah/master/tests/bud/run-scenarios/Dockerfile.entrypoint-run     79 B
Sending build context to Docker daemon 2.048 kB
Step 1/3 : FROM alpine
 ---> 3fd9065eaf02
Step 2/3 : ENTRYPOINT /invalid/entrypoint
 ---> Running in 1f3b80d35262
 ---> 66e47b60da01
Removing intermediate container 1f3b80d35262
Step 3/3 : RUN echo "unique.test.string"
 ---> Running in f9e15111d267

unique.test.string
 ---> 2566e18718b7
Removing intermediate container f9e15111d267
Successfully built 2566e18718b7
```

Buildah (fails without -f)
```
# buildah bud https://raw.githubusercontent.com/projectatomic/buildah/master/tests/bud/run-scenarios/Dockerfile.entrypoint-run
error prepping temporary context directory: Error processing tar file(exit status 1): unexpected EOF
```

-edit- Scratch that question 😄

Narrowing this down. It looks like the downloadToDirectory function always treats the source Dockerfile as a tar source.
```
# buildah --debug bud https://raw.githubusercontent.com/projectatomic/buildah/master/tests/bud/run-scenarios/Dockerfile.entrypoint-run                        
DEBU[0000] extracting "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/projectatomic/buildah/master/tests/bud/run-scenarios/Dockerfile.entrypoint-run" to "/tmp/buildah715107315" 
ERRO[0001] error prepping temporary context directory: Error processing tar file(exit status 1): unexpected EOF 
```

https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/1513b82eed96ff62c226a38cc9c8e25fc2911b23/imagebuildah/util.go#L37
The way it is supposed to work is it pulls the Dockerfile from the web site and then creates a tmp file with the Dockerfile in it and uses it from there?

I think it's more complicated than that: docker seems to be able to pull the URL and distinguish between a tarball, a git repo, and a simple Dockerfile. See `docker-build(1)` under DESCRIPTION:
```
    When the URL to a tarball archive or to a single Dockerfile is given,
    no context is sent from the client to the Docker daemon. In this case,
    the Dockerfile at the root of the archive and the rest of the archive will
    get used as the context of the build.  When a Git repository is set as
    the URL, the repository is cloned locally and then sent as the context.
```
Ok. @pixdrift if you want to run with this one, SGTM

@rhatdan, I would.. but I am at the point that I need assistance, what's the RH way to put my hand up?

Where I got to was above, with the extraction.

The code correctly detects that you provide an http(s) URL, but it then treats the URL as a tarball rather than just a flat Dockerfile.
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/1513b82eed96ff62c226a38cc9c8e25fc2911b23/imagebuildah/util.go#L74-L75

I think `downloadToDirectory` either needs a parameter to specify that the source type is compressed (eg. `extractSource=true`) or there needs to be a separate function defined, one for downloading Dockerfiles (eg. `downloadDockerfile`), and the existing one used for tarballs/images.

-edit-

I have also only found the above reference to `downloadToDirectory` in the source, which was surprising. Would there be any external components/consumers utilising it that aren't captured in this repo or is that unlikely for the util file?
@rhatdan, that looks great 👍. Will push another commit for something else I have outstanding and take a look.
Ok I just tried it out locally and it looks like it works now.

Issue is resolved. Additional CI tests added to confirm functionality:
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/tests/bud.bats#L569-L584
